creator: berend@pobox.com
text:
Same bug, but with rebuilt setup. Changed my root from ufs to zfs. Went
from i368 to amd62. My kernel is
https://svn0.us-west.freebsd.org/base/releng/9.1

Got exactly similar message.

Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: Fatal trap 12: page fault while in kernel mode
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: cpuid = 0; apic id = 00
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: fault virtual address	= 0x10
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: fault code		= supervisor read data, page
not present
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: instruction pointer	= 0x20:0xffffffff8174f8b8
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: stack pointer	        =
0x28:0xffffff81193bf380
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: frame pointer	        =
0x28:0xffffff81193bf390
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: code segment		= base 0x0, limit 0xfffff,
type 0x1b
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: = DPL 0, pres 1, long 1, def32 0, gran 1
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: processor eflags	= interrupt enabled,
resume, IOPL = 0
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: current process		= 2128 (named)
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: trap number		= 12
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: panic: page fault
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: cpuid = 0
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: KDB: stack backtrace:
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #0 0xffffffff8092a076 at kdb_backtrace+0x66
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #1 0xffffffff808f41cd at panic+0x1cd
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #2 0xffffffff80be8350 at trap_fatal+0x290
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #3 0xffffffff80be868d at trap_pfault+0x1ed
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #4 0xffffffff80be8cae at trap+0x3ce
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #5 0xffffffff80bd32bf at calltrap+0x8
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #6 0xffffffff81759e6f at pf_test_rule+0xf3f
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #7 0xffffffff8175d644 at pf_test+0xe34
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #8 0xffffffff817651c1 at pf_check_out+0x41
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #9 0xffffffff809b800e at pfil_run_hooks+0x9e
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #10 0xffffffff80a14806 at ip_output+0x416
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #11 0xffffffff80a91909 at udp_send+0x509
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #12 0xffffffff8095ec67 at sosend_dgram+0x2d7
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #13 0xffffffff80962f53 at kern_sendit+0x1a3
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #14 0xffffffff809631ec at sendit+0xdc
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #15 0xffffffff80963277 at sys_sendmsg+0x87
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #16 0xffffffff80be7bf6 at amd64_syscall+0x546
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: #17 0xffffffff80bd35a7 at Xfast_syscall+0xf7
Sep 20 14:31:59 bmach kernel: Uptime: 6h24m26s


My kernel is an exact copy of GENERIC + these additions:


options         MROUTING                # Multicast routing

# ALTQ
options         ALTQ
options         ALTQ_CBQ        # Class Bases Queuing (CBQ)
options         ALTQ_RED        # Random Early Detection (RED)
options         ALTQ_RIO        # RED In/Out
options         ALTQ_HFSC       # Hierarchical Packet Scheduler (HFSC)
options         ALTQ_CDNR
options         ALTQ_PRIQ       # Priority Queuing (PRIQ)
#options         ALTQ_NOPCC      # Required if the TSC is unusable
